title: functions in C++
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A function declares a return type, takes parameters, and returns a value.
explanation: twice adds its argument to itself. Calling twice(6) runs the body with n = 6 and hands back the result.

hints: twice adds the input to itself.

exercises.question:
Which keyword hands the value back to the caller?

_ n + n;
